title: 10 best herbal tinctures for hemorrhoids

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel tinctures helps with hemorrhoids because it contains astringent properties that help reduce inflammation and shrink swollen blood vessels. The antiseptic and antibacterial properties of witch hazel also aid in preventing infection and promoting healing. When taken internally as a tincture, witch hazel helps to soothe and calm the affected area, reducing discomfort and pain associated with hemorrhoids. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and ease tension around the anus, providing relief from itching and irritation.
remedy_properties: Astringent property: Witch Hazel's astringent property helps to reduce swelling, inflammation, and itching associated with hemorrhoids by constricting blood vessels and drying up excess fluid.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Witch Hazel, such as gallic acid and tannins, help to reduce pain, discomfort, and swelling caused by hemorrhoids by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antioxidant property: Witch Hazel's antioxidant properties help to protect the rectal tissue from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and irritation associated with hemorrhoids.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: Tannins help reduce inflammation and shrink swollen tissues in the rectal area, providing relief from hemorrhoid symptoms.
Flavonoids (specifically, Hamamelitannin): Hamamelitannin has astringent properties that help reduce blood flow to the affected area, reducing swelling and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.

Phenolic acids (specifically, Gallic acid): Gallic acid has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help soothe and protect the skin around the anus, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain a high concentration of astringent tannins and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and ease hemorrhoid symptoms.

Barks: The bark of Hamamelis virginiana contains a mixture of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can help to soothe and protect the affected area.

Roots: The roots of the plant contain a rich source of saponins, flavonoids, and tannins, which can help to reduce inflammation, promote healing, and provide relief from hemorrhoid pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh or dried hamamelis virginiana leaves and twigs in the fall or early spring.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to increase the surface area for extraction.
Combine 1 part chopped plant material with 2 parts 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another clean glass container, discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horse chestnut tinctures helps with hemorrhoids because it contains aescin, a powerful bioactive compound that strengthens blood vessel walls and improves circulation. This helps to reduce swelling and inflammation in the rectal area, alleviating discomfort and pain associated with hemorrhoids. Additionally, aescin's anti-inflammatory properties can help soothe and calm irritated tissue, promoting healing and reducing bleeding. As a natural remedy, horse chestnut tinctures offers a safe and effective way to manage symptoms of hemorrhoids without harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Aesculin: Aesculin has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, helping to reduce swelling and prevent infection in the affected area, thus alleviating discomfort and pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Escin: Escin is a bioflavonoid that helps to strengthen blood vessels and improve their elasticity, which can help to reduce bleeding and alleviate symptoms of internal hemorrhoids.

Quercetin: Quercetin is a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce swelling and inflammation in the rectal area, thus providing relief from itching, discomfort, and pain associated with hemorrhoids.
remedy_constituents: Hippocastanine: Acts as a vasoconstrictor, reducing blood flow to the affected area and thus alleviating swelling and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
Aesculetin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the rectal area caused by hemorrhoids.
Aesculin: Possesses astringent properties, which can help reduce bleeding and promote healing of the affected area by constricting blood vessels and preventing excessive fluid loss.
remedy_parts: Barks: The astringent and anti-inflammatory properties of the bark help reduce swelling and itching associated with hemorrhoids.
Seeds: The seeds of Aesculus hippocastanum contain aescin, a saponin compound that helps reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and promote healing in hemorrhoid cases.
Leaves: The leaves of the Horse Chestnut plant have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce bleeding and alleviate symptoms of hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Collect fresh or dried aesculus hippocastanum leaves and stems in sufficient quantities for tincture preparation.
Combine 1 part of the collected plant material with 2 parts of high-proof vodka in a clean glass container.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks shaking the container daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Allow the tincture to mature for 1 month before using it for medicinal purposes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tinctures helps with hemorrhoids because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The plantain plant has been traditionally used to treat various skin issues, including itching and swelling associated with hemorrhoids. When taken as a tincture, plantain extracts can help reduce inflammation and relieve discomfort caused by hemorrhoidal symptoms, such as pain, itching, and bleeding. Additionally, plantain's antioxidant properties may also aid in healing damaged tissues and promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tincture helps reduce swelling and inflammation associated with hemorrhoids, allowing for relief from pain and discomfort.

Antibacterial and Antiviral: Plantain's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial and viral infections that can exacerbate hemorrhoid symptoms, promoting healing and preventing further irritation.
Astringent: Plantain's astringent properties help constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding, providing temporary relief from hemorrhoidal bleeding and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Apolipoprotein A-I: It helps with hemorrhoids by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the anal region.
Flavonoids: They help with hemorrhoids by exerting anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can reduce swelling and promote healing.
Saponins: They help with hemorrhoids by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the anal region, which can alleviate discomfort and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in mucilage, which helps to soothe and protect the skin around hemorrhoids.
Roots: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Seeds: Rich in fatty acids and antioxidants that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing of hemorrhoid tissue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest plantago major leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plantago major leaves and flowers in a low-temperature dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 8 hours.
Combine 1 part dried plantago major leaves and flowers with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every 2 days.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discard the solids and label the bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with hemorrhoids because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The mucilages present in marshmallow root create a protective barrier on the affected area, reducing swelling and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids. Additionally, marshmallow's antioxidant properties help to combat oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by chronic constipation or straining during bowel movements. remedy_properties: Mucoactive and emollient properties: Marshmallow tincture helps soothe and protect irritated tissues, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids by coating and lubricating the affected area.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The tannins and flavonoids present in marshmallow tincture have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling, pain, and itching caused by hemorrhoids.

Wound healing and tissue regeneration properties: Marshmallow tincture's high mucilage content can promote wound healing by creating a protective barrier that allows damaged tissues to repair themselves, reducing the risk of further irritation and discomfort.

Polysaccharides: Polysaccharides, particularly galactose and rhamnose, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and pain in the rectal area.

Althaea flavonoids: Althaea flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Althaea officinalis are commonly used to make tinctures for hemorrhoids due to their high mucilage content, which helps soothe and protect irritated tissue.
Leaves: The leaves of Althaea officinalis are used to make tinctures for hemorrhoids because they contain flavonoids and phenolic compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: The flowers of Althaea officinalis are used to make tinctures for hemorrhoids due to their content of mucilages and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of roots by digging them up in early spring or fall when above ground parts are dormant.
Clean the roots by gently brushing off dirt and debris with a soft-bristled brush for 5 minutes.
Dry the roots by spreading them out in a single layer on a paper bag for 1 week.
Prepare the dried roots by cutting them into small pieces and weighing 1 part of the dried roots to 2 parts of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the dried roots in the vodka for 2 weeks with occasional shaking of the jar every day.

remedy_parts: Buds: They are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and reduce swelling in hemorrhoids.
Leaves: The leaves of Tilia europaea contain flavonoids and tannins, which have astringent and anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce bleeding and itching associated with hemorrhoids.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of fresh tilia europaea leaves and flowers and 2 parts of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks to allow the flavors to infuse.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ulmus rubra leaves and twigs in early morning when dew is still present on the plant for optimal potency.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves when in full bloom and dry them for 2-3 weeks.
Combine the dried material with 500 milliliters of 50 percent ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part of Calendula officinalis for tinctures due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Leaves are also used in tincture production due to their similar bioactive compound profile to the flowers, providing anti-inflammatory and soothing effects.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of dried calendula flowers and add to a clean glass jar with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks to allow the mixture to infuse.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Label and date the bottle and store it in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks to mature.
Shake the bottle well every day during the 4 to 6 week maturation period to ensure even extraction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with hemorrhoids because of its soothing, anti-inflammatory properties that ease discomfort and reduce swelling. The herbal extract relaxes the muscles in the rectal area, decreasing pain and itching associated with hemorrhoids. Additionally, chamomile's natural antispasmodic properties help to calm down the rectal nerves, reducing spasms and promoting a sense of calm. remedy_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid compound found in Matricaria chamomilla, apigenin has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.

remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in flavonoids and essential oils, which are believed to provide anti-inflammatory and soothing effects that help alleviate hemorrhoid symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of matricaria chamomilla flowers in the morning when they are dry and free of moisture.
Clean the flowers by gently shaking them to remove dirt and debris from the surface.
Use a 1:2 ratio of plant material to solvent and combine 100g of matricaria chamomilla flowers with 200ml of 80% ethanol.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional agitation to ensure proper extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids to produce a clear liquid.

remedy_recipe: Harvest urtica dioica leaves and stems in the morning when dew is still present for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using shade or low-temperature dehydration methods within 24 hours of collection.
Grind 30 grams of dried urtica dioica into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered herb with 60% vodka in a clean glass jar, covering the contents by at least an inch for maceration.

Store the jar in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking daily to facilitate extraction, then strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter before transferring it to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red-seeded dandelion tinctures helps with hemorrhoids because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. Cleanse the harvested roots by washing them gently with distilled water and removing any debris.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces weighing 10% of the total amount for tincture production and prepare for maceration.
Combine the chopped roots with 70% ethanol in a ratio of 1:5 and seal the container for 2 weeks of steeping at room temperature.
Filter the mixture using cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate the liquid from the solids and discard the solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried frangula alnus bark, ensuring it is free from contaminants and pesticides.
Weigh 100 grams of the bark and combine it with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily during this time.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.

remedy_recipe: Gather 500 grams of dried berberis vulgaris root bark and 95% ethanol for solvent.
Grind 250 grams of the berberis vulgaris root bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine the powdered root bark with 950 milliliters of the 95% ethanol in a glass jar to create a mixture.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Agrimony tincture helps to reduce swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that promote inflammation.

Astringent properties: The astringent compounds in Agrimony tincture help to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding from hemorrhoids, promoting healing and reducing the risk of further irritation.
Antioxidant properties: Agrimony tincture contains antioxidants that help to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to the development of hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are valued for their ability to reduce inflammation and pain associated with hemorrhoids, making them an effective ingredient in herbal tinctures.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that help to soothe and calm irritated tissue, reducing symptoms of hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried agrimonia eupatoria leaves and flowers in sufficient quantity for the desired batch size.
Combine one part agrimonia eupatoria herb with three parts vodka or other neutral spirit in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a dark place at room temperature for six to eight weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles, label with date and ingredients, and store in a cool dark place for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Butterbur tinctures helps with hemorrhoids because it has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. The herb's flavonoids and sesquiterpenes work to constrict blood vessels, which alleviates pressure on the hemorrhoidal tissues. Additionally, butterbur's soothing effects can help calm irritated skin and mucous membranes, promoting a sense of comfort and relief from itching and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids. As a natural remedy, butterbur tinctures provide an effective alternative to harsh chemicals and pharmaceuticals for managing this common condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Butterbur tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as sesquiterpenes, help reduce swelling and inflammation in the affected areas, providing relief from pain, discomfort, and itching associated with hemorrhoids.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of butterbur tincture help protect the blood vessels in the rectal area from oxidative damage, which can contribute to the development and worsening of hemorrhoids.

Vasodilatory effects: Butterbur tincture's vasodilatory compounds may help dilate blood vessels, reducing pressure on the veins and improving circulation around the anus, which can alleviate symptoms like pain, bleeding, and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties and can help soothe irritated tissues and reduce swelling around the rectal area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh petasites hybridus leaves and flowers in the morning when dew is still present on the plants.
Rinse the harvested material with cold filtered water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine 250 grams of rinsed petasites hybridus with 1000 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and place it in a cool dark area for 6-8 weeks, shaking the mixture twice daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids, and store it in the refrigerator at 40 degrees fahrenheit.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow gentian tinctures helps with hemorrhoids because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The herb's active compounds, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, help to reduce swelling and itching associated with hemorrhoids, providing instant relief from discomfort. Additionally, yellow gentian's astringent nature helps to constrict blood vessels, which can alleviate bleeding and reduce the risk of further irritation. As a natural remedy, yellow gentian tinctures offer a gentle yet effective way to soothe and heal hemorrhoids, promoting overall digestive health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yellow Gentian tincture helps reduce swelling and inflammation associated with hemorrhoids, which can alleviate pain and discomfort.
Astringent: The astringent property of yellow gentian helps shrink and constrict blood vessels, reducing bleeding and relieving pressure on the anal area.
Antioxidant: Yellow Gentian's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and free radical damage in the body, which can contribute to hemorrhoidal symptoms such as itching and irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Rhyzomes: Contains a high concentration of alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can provide relief from hemorrhoidal symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried gentiana lutea flowers and leaves in sufficient quantities for the desired dosage.
Weigh out one part of the herb material and combine it with two parts of high-proof vodka in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks shaking the jar daily for optimal extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Label and store the finished gentiana lutea tincture in a cool dark place using it as directed on the label.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for hemorrhoids are concentrated liquid extracts made from plants that have anti-inflammatory, soothing, and astringent properties. These tinctures help to reduce swelling, ease pain, and promote healing in the affected area. Examples of herbal tinctures that help with hemorrhoids include witch hazel, which reduces inflammation and relieves itching, and aloe vera, which soothes and calms irritated tissue. By using these tinctures, individuals can alleviate symptoms such as discomfort, itching, and bleeding, allowing them to resume their daily activities with renewed comfort and confidence.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hemorrhoids that are treated with herbal tinctures are rectal fissures, anal fistulas, and perianal pruritus. These conditions share symptoms such as itching, burning, and discomfort in the genital area. Herbal tinctures containing ingredients like witch hazel, goldenseal, and plantain can help soothe and calm irritated tissues, reduce inflammation, and promote healing. They may also be used to treat rectal bleeding and pain associated with these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with hemorrhoids is a blend of witch hazel, calendula, and aloe vera. Witch hazel tincture helps reduce inflammation and soothe irritation, while calendula tincture promotes healing and tissue repair. Aloe vera tincture provides moisture and calms the skin, reducing discomfort and pain. Taking a combination of these tinctures, typically 10-15 drops each, 2-3 times a day, may help alleviate symptoms and promote healing of hemorrhoids. Consult a healthcare professional before using herbal remedies.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of integrative medicine, herbal tinctures can be a good option for hemorrhoids due to the presence of numerous plants with hemorrhoid-healing properties in Iranian traditional medicine (ITM). The study, which reviewed 37 medicinal plants with hemorrhoid-healing effects, identified six species (Allium ampeloprasum, Phyllanthus emblica, Aloe vera, Terminalia chebula, Vitis vinifera, and Commiphora mukul) with the largest number of related pharmacological effects documented in scientific databases. By utilizing these herbal remedies, individuals may be able to manage hemorrhoids more effectively, suggesting the potential benefits of herbal tinctures in this context.
